hero.title: Staten Island Society of
hero.subtitle: Building Community, One Track at a Time
hero.description: For nearly 70 years, we’ve brought together hobbyists of all ages to build, operate, and share the art of model railroading. Whether you’re a seasoned modeler or just curious, there’s a place for you on our layout.
hero.heroImageAlt: Staten Island #482 HO-scale locomotive model on detailed track with scenery

about.title: A Staten Island Tradition Since 1956
about.lead: The Staten Island Society of Model Railroaders, Inc. is one of Staten Island’s longest-standing and most beloved hobby clubs.
paragraphs: First chartered in 1956, our club has called several Staten Island locations home — from our origins in Pleasant Plains, to Historic Richmond Town, to over a decade at Sea View Hospital Rehabilitation Center.

Our members combine craft, imagination, and engineering to recreate miniature worlds showcasing models of actual American trains. Using the popular HO-scale (1:87 ratio), each member builds their own module — a 2×4-foot section of railroad — that connects with others to form sprawling, detailed layouts.
about.quote: “This is a hobby that lends itself to always being worked on. You can always add to it or subtract as you get new ideas and more products come on the market.”

timeline.title: Club Founded
timeline.text: Chartered in Pleasant Plains, Staten Island, bringing together model railroad enthusiasts.

timeline.title: Richmond Town Era
timeline.text: Relocated to Historic Richmond Town, growing the club and its community presence.

timeline.title: Sea View Hospital
timeline.text: Moved to the Staff House at Sea View, running monthly shows for residents and the community.

timeline.title: Looking for a New Home
timeline.text: After building renovations at Sea View, the club seeks a new permanent meeting space.

timeline.title: Still Chugging Along
timeline.text: 28+ members strong and actively meeting, doing shows, and welcoming newcomers.

activities.title: Something for Everyone
activities.subtitle: Model railroading is more than a hobby — it’s art, engineering, history, and community rolled into one.

cards.title: Build & Operate Layouts
cards.text: Members build individual modules (2×4 ft, HO-scale) that connect to form massive, detailed railroad layouts with lifelike scenery, bridges, towns, and rolling stock.

cards.title: Community Shows
cards.text: We set up free model railroad displays at schools, churches, and cultural institutions across Staten Island — sharing our passion and bringing smiles to kids and families.

cards.title: Holiday Train Shows
cards.text: Our annual Holiday Train Show is a beloved Staten Island tradition, drawing families from across the borough to marvel at miniature worlds in motion.

cards.title: Buy, Sell & Trade
cards.text: Members participate in buy-and-sell events across the tri-state area, finding rare pieces and sharing treasures with fellow enthusiasts in New Jersey and beyond.

cards.title: Workshop & Learning
cards.text: From beginners running their first set to experts building trains from scratch, we share skills in scenery, wiring, weathering, and scratch-building.

cards.title: Fellowship
cards.text: More than trains — it’s the friendships. Members meet regularly to work together, share stories, and enjoy a creative hobby that spans generations.

news.title: Making Headlines on Staten Island

articles.headline: Bounced from longtime meeting place, model railroad hobbyist club in search of a new home
articles.excerpt: “We want a place where we could have a workshop, we want a place where we can set up layouts. We were contributing to that environment.”

articles.headline: Members of Staten Island Society of Model Railroaders have long and rich history
articles.excerpt: “When you build something, you learn so much. It’s not just what you’re building, it’s all that goes with it. A diorama has scale, proportion, placement.”

articles.headline: Model trains on center stage at annual holiday show
articles.excerpt: The annual Holiday Train Show at Clove Lakes, a joint venture with the Richmond County Model Railroad Club, draws families from across the borough.

news.featuredQuote: “Any hobby should be relaxing. It takes a lot of thought to make the modules look simple and make it look realistic. When it’s finished, there’s a great deal of satisfaction.”

gallery.title: Miniature Worlds, Major Detail
gallery.subtitle: From tiny telephone poles to intricate bridges, every module tells a story.

cta.title: We’re Looking for a New Home
cta.text: After more than a decade at Sea View Hospital, our club needs a permanent space where we can set up layouts, hold workshops, and welcome the community. If you know of a space on Staten Island, we’d love to hear from you.

join.title: All Aboard!
join.subtitle: Whether you’ve been collecting trains since childhood or you’re just discovering the hobby, we welcome you.
